30.reason: [Error: When right1 is 0, setRight() sets right to 1, but right1 is not updated, so the Word object is still created with right1=0, causing the error to be recorded even if the lexer state is reset. Additionally, the logic for detecting single '&' or '|' errors is flawed because the error flag is reset prematurely.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: When right1 is 0, setRight() sets right to 1, but right1 is not updated, so the Word object is still created with right1=0, causing the error to be recorded even if the lexer state is reset. Additionally, the logic for detecting single '&' or '|' errors is flawed because the error flag is reset prematurely.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: After detecting an error (right1 == 0), the code calls lexer.setRight() which sets right to 1, but this resets the error flag for the current token, causing the error to be lost. The right variable in Compiler is set to 0, but the lexer's internal right is reset to 1, so subsequent tokens will not have the error flag properly propagated.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Resets the lexer's error flag to 1, losing the error detection for subsequent tokens. Should not reset it.
